name: Streamarr
description: Monitor Twitch channels and YouTube streams for live content, auto-create/remove channels. Dual detection: Twitch Helix API (fast batch) or streamlink CLI fallback. Supports cookie-based authentication for age-restricted or subscriber-only streams.

settings.label: Core Setup
settings.description: Basic Twitch monitoring and playlist assignment.

fields.label: Monitored Channels (Twitch & YouTube)
fields.helper_text:
One entry per line. Lines starting with # are ignored.

Twitch: username or username=BaseNumber (for decimal numbering)
  nightbot
  pokimane=50

YouTube: full URL to a live channel or stream
  https://www.youtube.com/@ChannelHandle
  https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xxxx

YouTube detection uses streamlink and supports live streams only. Cookies from the linked Stream Profile are automatically passed to streamlink.

⚠ Without Twitch API credentials, each Twitch channel is checked individually via streamlink. This is slow for more than ~20 channels.

fields.label: Stream Profile (streamlink)
fields.helper_text:
Select a Stream Profile with the 'Streamlink' backend. FFmpeg profiles will NOT work for Twitch streams.

⚠ You must first create a Stream Profile under Settings → Stream Profiles with backend set to 'Streamlink' and quality 'best'. Cookies configured on this profile will also be used for stream detection.

fields.label: Target Playlist (Standard)
fields.helper_text: Standard playlist to associate created channels with. Leave empty to skip.

fields.label: Target Playlist (Custom)
fields.helper_text: Custom playlist to add created channels to. Leave empty to skip.

settings.label: Live & Grouping
settings.description: How live channels are titled, grouped and shown in the EPG.

fields.label: Group Mode
fields.helper_text: Static uses the fixed group name below. Game mode groups live channels by their current Twitch game/category (e.g. 'Just Chatting', 'Fortnite'). Game mode requires Twitch API credentials and falls back to static without them. VOD channels always use the VOD Group name.

fields.label: Live Channel Group
fields.helper_text: Group name for live-stream channels. Used in static mode, and as fallback in game mode when the game is unknown.

fields.label: EPG Mode

options.game: Game / Category (shows game changes as programme entries)
options.title: Stream Title (single programme with the stream title)

fields.helper_text: Game mode creates a new programme entry each time the streamer switches games. Title mode shows a single continuous programme using the stream title — useful if you prefer all channels under one group with simpler EPG.

fields.label: Live Channel Title Mode
fields.helper_text: Controls the title of live channels. Stream Title uses the current Twitch stream title. Channel Name uses the Twitch channel display name.

fields.label: VOD Group
fields.helper_text: Group name for VOD channels.

fields.label: YouTube Live Group
fields.helper_text: Group name for YouTube live-stream channels. YouTube channels are always grouped here, regardless of Group Mode.

settings.label: Content & Cleanup
settings.description: Playback quality, VOD imports and cleanup behavior.

fields.label: Stream Quality
fields.helper_text: Preferred stream quality. Streamlink will select the closest available quality.

fields.label: Output Format
fields.helper_text: Container format for created channels. Must match the format configured on the linked Stream Profile. Changing this setting updates all existing channels on the next Check Now.

fields.label: Include VODs
fields.helper_text: Also create channels for recent VODs of monitored channels. Requires Twitch API credentials - VOD discovery is not available via streamlink fallback.

fields.label: VOD Limit per Channel
fields.helper_text: Maximum number of recent VODs to import per monitored Twitch channel.

fields.label: Auto-cleanup Ended Streams
fields.helper_text: Automatically delete live-stream channels when the stream ends. Runs during each monitoring cycle. VOD channels are never auto-cleaned.

settings.label: Numbering & Xtream
settings.description: Channel numbering strategy and Xtream client compatibility options.

fields.label: Starting Channel Number
fields.helper_text: First channel number to assign. Each new stream increments from here. For best Xtream client compatibility, keep this in a low range (for example 900-999).

fields.label: Channel Number Increment
fields.helper_text: How much to increment the channel number for each new stream.

fields.label: Channel Numbering Mode
fields.helper_text: Decimal mode groups streams from the same Twitch channel using the channel's BaseNumber. Sequential mode uses a global counter.

fields.label: Xtream Compatibility Mode
fields.helper_text: When enabled, Streamarr keeps channel numbers in a bounded range to avoid clients that hide high channel numbers.

fields.label: Xtream Min Channel Number
fields.helper_text: Lower bound for Xtream compatibility numbering.

fields.label: Xtream Max Channel Number
fields.helper_text: Upper bound for Xtream compatibility numbering. Existing plugin channels outside this range are auto-renumbered on Check Now.

fields.label: Xtream ASCII Text Compatibility
fields.helper_text: Removes emoji and problematic special characters from Streamarr channel names/titles to improve compatibility with strict Xtream clients.

settings.label: Twitch API
settings.description: Optional Twitch Helix API credentials for faster detection and VOD imports.

fields.label: Twitch Client ID
fields.helper_text: Twitch API Client ID for fast batch detection. Get one at https://dev.twitch.tv/console/apps - create an application and copy the Client ID. Leave empty to use streamlink fallback.

fields.label: Twitch Client Secret
fields.helper_text: Twitch API Client Secret. Required together with Client ID for API-based detection.

fields.label: Enable Automatic Monitoring
fields.helper_text: Run monitoring automatically on the schedule below. Requires the scheduler to be running (php artisan schedule:run).

fields.label: Monitor Schedule (cron)
fields.helper_text: Cron expression for automatic monitoring. Default: every 10 minutes. Examples: */5 * * * * (every 5 min), 0 * * * * (hourly).

fields.label: Twitch URL(s)
fields.helper_text: One or more Twitch channel or VOD URLs (newline or comma-separated). Examples: https://twitch.tv/pokimane, https://twitch.tv/videos/123456789

fields.label: Stream URL
fields.helper_text: Any URL supported by streamlink (e.g. https://twitch.tv/pokimane or https://www.youtube.com/@ChannelHandle). Returns live status, metadata, and available stream qualities. Cookies from the configured Stream Profile are automatically used.
